title: hibbertia cistiflora subsp. rostrata
intro: Hibbertia cistiflora subsp. rostrata, also known as the 'Banksia Leaved Guinea Flower', is a flowering plant native to Western Australia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have shown potential in treating various health conditions. The plant is believed to have numerous health benefits, including reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and acting as an antioxidant. Hibbertia cistiflora subsp. rostrata can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for its medicinal properties, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it as a treatment.
